name: Getting Started with the Genesis Design System
description: From clone to first page in under ten minutes. Install the theme, pick a layout, and let the ontological engine handle typography, spacing, color, and responsive behavior.

step.name: Clone the Repository
step.text: Clone the theme repository from GitHub to your local machine.

step.name: Install Dependencies
step.text: Install Ruby gems required by Jekyll and the theme.

step.name: Serve Locally
step.text: Start the Jekyll development server to preview the theme.

step.name: Create Your First Page
step.text: Create a new file with YAML front matter specifying the layout you want to use.

itemListElement.text: Create a file with front matter: layout: post, title: 'Hello World'. Write your content below the front matter block. The layout handles typography, spacing, and responsive behavior.

step.name: Choose a Layout
step.text: Select from 20+ production layouts: post, article, landing, dashboard, docs, gallery, chatroom, and more. Each layout is responsive and accessible out of the box.

step.name: Customize with Ontology
step.text: For advanced styling, use ontological SCSS mixins. Map your HTML elements to semantic categories: genesis-environment for layout, genesis-entity for surfaces, genesis-cognition for typography, genesis-synapse for interactions.
